from fastapi import FastAPI, Request, WebSocket from fastapi.responses import HTMLResponse from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles import json app = FastAPI() # Configuración para servir archivos estáticos (como CSS, JS, etc.) app.mount("/static", StaticFiles(directory="static"), name="static") # Variable para almacenar el último dato recibido del Arduino latest_data = {} # Ruta para recibir los datos JSON del Arduino @app.post("/data") async def receive_data(data: dict): global latest_data latest_data = data return {"message": "Data received successfully"} # Ruta para mostrar el valor más reciente en una página HTML @app.get("/", response_class=HTMLResponse) async def show_data(request: Request): return """ Arduino Data

Arduino Data

# extracted_signals from fastapi import FastAPI, Request, WebSocket from fastapi.responses import HTMLResponse from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles import json app = FastAPI()

# Usa la imagen base de Python
FROM python:3.10

# Establece el directorio de trabajo en /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copia los archivos de requerimientos al contenedor
COPY requirements.txt .

# Instala las dependencias del proyecto
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copia todo el código al contenedor
COPY . .

# Define el comando por defecto para ejecutar tu aplicación
CMD ["python", "run.py"]

# extracted_signals
CMD ["python", "run.py"]

blueprint.one_liner: Python FastAPI API service for Arduino data.
blueprint.title: README Blueprint
blueprint.tagline: A concise guide to setting up and understanding the 'distancia-wemos' project.
blueprint.overview: This project is a Python FastAPI API service designed to handle data received from an Arduino device. It includes a Dockerfile for containerization, a README for documentation, and a static directory for serving static files like CSS and HTML.
architecture: The project is structured with a single entry point in the 'app.py' file.
It uses FastAPI for building the API and includes a Static files directory for serving static content.
The 'Dockerfile' is used to build a Docker image for the project.
The 'index.html' file is a static HTML page that displays the latest data received from the Arduino.
The 'README.md' file provides detailed instructions and information about the project.
installation: To install the project, clone the repository and run `pip install -r requirements.txt` to install dependencies.
The Dockerfile can be used to build a Docker image with the command `docker build -t distancia-wemos .`.
The project requires a running Docker container to be accessible via a URL.
usage: To run the project, use the command `docker run -p 8000:80 distancia-wemos` to start a container and expose port 8000.
The API can be tested using tools like Postman or curl to send POST requests to `/data` with JSON data.
The static HTML page can be accessed via the URL exposed by the Docker container.
testing: The project does not include any tests, so manual verification is recommended.
No automated testing framework is integrated into the project.
docker: The Dockerfile is provided to build a Docker image for the project.
The Docker image can be run with the command `docker run -p 8000:80 distancia-wemos` to expose port 80.
repository_notes: The project has a Git repository with the files structured as shown.
No CI/CD pipeline is set up, so manual deployment is required.
next_steps: To enhance the project, consider adding more routes, improving error handling, and adding unit tests.
For deployment, use the Docker image built from the Dockerfile to deploy the application.
